mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound for a sustained period keeps the equity discount rate compressed, supporting valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as no tightening signal emerges.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 as of 2014-04, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are not significantly higher during near-zero rate periods versus tightening periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below the historically referenced 6.5% policy threshold, markets reprice expectations of earlier monetary tightening, producing a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns as discount rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% for the first time in the cycle
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.3 as of 2014-04, near the 6.5% Evans-rule threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE crossing below 6.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate of negative returns is below 55% across historical crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based consumer demand upswing that historically precedes stronger corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month; the effect requires both series to co-move, since either alone is noisy.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month change > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=434571 and HOUST=946 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series RSAFS-only model achieves equal or higher Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth during a mid-cycle expansion signals healthy capacity utilization and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship reverses late in the cycle when overheating triggers tightening fears.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=102.7435 as of 2014-04 showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (correlation flips sign or Rank IC < 0.03) once FEDFUNDS begins a sustained tightening trend, without the hypothesis explicitly flagging regime change.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payroll employment growth reflects expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=138252 thousand as of 2014-04, trending upward
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above the Federal Reserve's implicit 2% comfort threshold, near-term rate hike expectations rise abruptly, causing a short-horizon negative shock to S&P 500 returns as discount rates are repriced upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI threshold crossings are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or occur less than 50% of the time across historical crossing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales growth indicates broad-based aggregate demand strength that compounds into sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; the effect is interaction-driven since GDP alone is too lagged and low-frequency to time returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 3%
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1-RSAFS trigger episodes show 60-day forward returns not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05), or GDPC1 revision volatility materially degrades signal stability across vintages.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound monetary regime, declining unemployment is interpreted as pure labor market healing without near-term tightening risk, jointly with near-zero FEDFUNDS this combination produces positive S&P 500 forward returns; the same UNRATE decline in a rate-hiking regime instead signals imminent policy tightening and reverses the sign.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines >0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.3 declining trend, FEDFUNDS=0.09 as of 2014-04
failure_condition: Hit rate of positive 20-day forward returns under the joint trigger falls below 55%, or the same UNRATE decline pattern under a tightening FEDFUNDS regime fails to show a sign reversal in returns.
